differential_diagnoses: Meconium aspiration syndrome with air leak complications
Persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn (PPHN)
Severe neonatal pneumonia with pulmonary abscess
next_steps: Perform contrast-enhanced computed tomography (CT) of the chest to characterize the persistent opacity and evaluate for anatomical anomalies
Perform flexible fiberoptic bronchoscopy to rule out endobronchial obstruction or airway malformation
Obtain microbiological cultures via bronchoalveolar lavage to guide antimicrobial therapy for suspected pneumonia
Monitor oxygenation trends and perform serial echocardiography to assess right ventricular function and pulmonary pressures
Maintain high-frequency oscillatory ventilation (HFOV) or consider ECMO if respiratory failure becomes refractory to conventional ventilation
